---
title: "report(): more examples"
---
<br>

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
    error = TRUE, 
    collapse = FALSE,
    comment = "##"
)
```

```{r setup}
library(saferDev)
```
<br>

### Dataset
```{r}
string1 <- "THIS IS A LOG"
vec1 <- 1:6 # vector of integers.
vec2 <- letters[1:9] # vector of characters.
mat1 <- matrix(vec1, nrow = 2, ncol = 3, byrow = TRUE) # matrix of integer having 2 rows and 3 columns.
dimnames(mat1) <- list(c("Row 1","Row 2"), c("C1", "C2", "C3"))
mat2 <- mat1
mode(mat2) <- "character"
t1 <- table(mat1) # 1D table.
vec3 <- as.vector(t1)
names(vec3) <- letters[1:6] # vector with names.
t2 <- table(vec2, vec2) # 2D table.
l1 <- list(vec1, vec2) # list.
```
<br>

### Dataset info
```{r}
string1 # string.
vec1 # vector of integers.
vec2 # vector of characters.
vec3 # vector of integers with names. No more [1] in front of values.
mat1 # matrix of integers with the row names.
mat2 # matrix of characters with the row names.
t1 # 1D table.
t2 # 2D table.
l1 # list.
```
<br>

### Simple examples
```{r}
# Print the input into the output file in the indicated path.
report(data = string1, path = ".")
# Idem.
report(data = vec1, path = ".")
# Idem.
report(data = vec2, path = ".")
# Idem.
report(data = vec3, path = ".")
# Idem.
report(data = mat1, path = ".")
# Idem. Of note, we cannot see that it is mode character (see noquote below).
report(data = mat2, path = ".")
# Idem.
report(data = t1, path = ".")
# Idem.
report(data = t2, path = ".")
# Idem.
report(data = l1, path = ".")
```
<br>

### Argument <code>output</code>
```{r, echo = TRUE, error = TRUE}
# Print the vector vec1 into the output file named "test.txt" in the path.
report(data = vec1, output = "test.txt", path = ".")
```
<br>

### Argument <code>overwrite</code>
```{r, echo = TRUE, error = TRUE}
# If the file exists, the output file content will be erased before printing.
report(data = vec2, overwrite = TRUE, output = "test.txt", path = ".")
```
<br>

### Argument <code>rownames_kept</code>
```{r, echo = TRUE, error = TRUE}
 # Keep the row names when print the matrix mat1 in the file.
report(
  data = mat1, 
  rownames_kept = TRUE, 
  path = "."
)
# Remove the row names.
report(
  data = mat1, 
  rownames_kept = FALSE, 
  path = "."
)
# Keep the row names when printing the table t1 in the file.
report(
  data = t1, 
  rownames_kept = TRUE, 
  path = "."
) 
# Remove the row names.
report(
  data = t1, 
  rownames_kept = FALSE, 
  path = "."
)
# Keep the row names when print the table t2 in the file.
report(
  data = t2, 
  rownames_kept = TRUE, 
  path = "."
)
# Remove the row names.
report(
  data = t2, 
  rownames_kept = FALSE, 
  path = "."
) 

```
<br>

### Argument <code>vector_cat</code>
```{r, echo = TRUE, error = TRUE}
# Print a vector of length > 1 using cat() instead of capture.output(). Names are lost.
report(
  data = vec3, 
  vector_cat = TRUE, 
  path = "."
)
```
<br>

### Argument <code>noquote</code>
```{r, echo = TRUE, error = TRUE}
# Quotes are kept for the characters.
report(
  data = mat2, 
  noquote = FALSE, 
  path = "."
)
```
<br>

### Argument <code>sep</code>
```{r, echo = TRUE, error = TRUE}
# 4 lines added after printed data.
report(
  data = vec1, 
  sep = 4, 
  path = "."
)
```
<br>

### Argument <code>safer_check</code>
```{r, echo = TRUE, error = TRUE}
# Safer checkings are performed before main code running 
report(
    data = vec1, 
    safer_check = TRUE
)
```
<br>

### Argument <code>lib_path</code>
```{r}
# Error returned because the R required packages are not in the "." path.
report(
    data = vec1, 
    lib_path = "."
)
```
<br>

### Argument <code>error_text</code>
```{r}
# Add a text in error messages returned by report().
report(
    data = vec1, 
    lib_path = ".", 
    error_text = " === TEXT ADDED ==="
)
```
<br>

### All the arguments with their descriptions
```{r, echo = TRUE, error = TRUE}
# matrix mat1 will be print in the file "text2.txt" in the path, with quote and row names being kept, and 4 lines after printing data
report(
    data = mat2, 
    output = "test2.txt", 
    path = ".", 
    overwrite = TRUE, 
    rownames_kept = TRUE, 
    vector_cat = TRUE, 
    noquote = FALSE, 
    sep = 4,
    safer_check = TRUE, # Single logical value. Perform some "safer" checks (see https://github.com/safer-r)?
    lib_path = NULL, # absolute pathways of the directories containing the required packages if not in the default directories.
    error_text = "" # add information in error messages returned by the function.
)

```
